location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in-office; Wednesday optional WFH; Friday depends on role/team

level_signals: High-autonomy, high-impact framing: 'you create the structure rather than waiting for it'
Comp range $194K-$220K base consistent with Senior/Staff IC at Asana
Thought partner to security leadership on org design

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 8+ years of TPM experience, with meaningful time in a security context (security engineering, GRC, product security, infra security, or similar)
must_haves.qualifier: with meaningful time in a security context

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Strong understanding of security with the ability to engage technical teams and communicate clearly to executive stakeholders
Demonstrated ability to lead large-scale, cross-functional programs from ambiguous inception through delivery
Exceptional written and verbal communication, with proven ability to influence without authority at all levels
Track record of operating with high autonomy — you create the structure rather than waiting for it

scope.surface: Security organization-wide operating model: planning cycles, KPI frameworks, dashboards, cross-functional programs
primary_metrics_inferred: Cross-functional stakeholder alignment on security priorities
core_responsibilities: Lead high-impact, cross-functional special projects spanning organizational boundaries
Own the Security organization's annual and quarterly planning cycles
Drive prioritization and communication of security asks across stakeholders
Define and own the Security team's KPI framework covering risk posture, program health, and strategic goal progress
Build dashboards and reporting cadences for leadership visibility
Establish and continuously improve operating cadences (All Hands, program reviews, OKR check-ins)
Act as thought partner to security leadership on org design, process improvement, and operational scale

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed ($194K-$220K base) and competitive
Explicitly states 'if you're interested and don't meet every listed requirement, we still encourage you to apply'
High autonomy and high-impact framing — not a execution-only role
Clear articulation of scope and responsibilities
Asana has a strong brand and consistent workplace culture recognition (Fortune Best Workplaces 7+ years)
red_flags: In-office 3 days per week (Mon/Tue/Thu) in San Francisco — not suitable for remote candidates
Visa sponsorship not mentioned; unclear whether available
No nice-to-haves listed — hard requirements may be strictly enforced
Security context requirement is meaningful but vaguely scoped ('meaningful time') — could be interpreted strictly or loosely

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range + autonomy signals + 8+ years requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from Asana's product description as SaaS platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly listed as metrics)
location.visa_sponsorship set to 'unclear' — not mentioned in JD
ambiguities: Friday WFH eligibility is conditional ('depends on the type of work you do and the teams with which you partner') — actual hybrid schedule may vary from the Mon/Tue/Thu standard
'Meaningful time in a security context' is not quantified — ambiguous as to how many years in security are required vs total TPM years
